title: Zurich club T&M closes after more than 25 years
description: The T&M, a fixture of Zurich's gay nightlife for over a quarter century, shut its doors on 24 February 2013.

title: Club Heaven opens in Zurich
description: A new club named Heaven opened in Zurich on 8 March 2013, adding a fresh venue to the city's queer nightlife.

title: Zurich youth group Tankschtell takes the name Spot25
description: On 19 March 2013 the Zurich queer youth group Tankschtell adopted the name Spot25, reviving the traditional name of the earlier HAZ youth group.

title: LGBT police association pinkcop marks five years
description: pinkcop, the Swiss association of LGBT police officers, celebrated its fifth anniversary on 7 June 2013 in the presence of two members of Zurich's city executive along with international guests.

title: Zurich Pride Festival 2013 centers rainbow families
description: The 2013 Zurich Pride Festival ran from 7 to 9 June under a motto declaring that every family counts equally, putting rainbow families at the heart of the program.

title: Geneva unveils a memorial to Bartholomé Tecia, drowned in 1566
description: On 10 June 2013 Geneva dedicated a plaque at the spot on the Rhone where the Piedmontese student Bartholomé Tecia was drowned in 1566 on a sodomy conviction, his confession having been extracted under torture.

title: Green Liberal group files the Ehe für Alle marriage initiative
description: On 5 December 2013 the Green Liberal parliamentary group submitted an initiative in the Swiss federal parliament calling for civil marriage to be opened to same-sex couples, starting the legislative process that would eventually lead to marriage equality.

title: Swiss film Der Kreis premieres at the Berlinale
description: Stefan Haupt's docudrama Der Kreis, portraying the Zurich circle around the mid-century gay journal of the same name, premiered at the Berlin film festival in February 2014 to excellent reviews, winning the Teddy Award for best documentary essay film plus the audience prize. The source timeline files the premiere under 10 January.

title: Marriage equality rally held in Bern on IDAHOT
description: Campaigners gathered on Bern's Münsterplatz on 17 May 2014, the International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia, Interphobia and Transphobia, to demand the opening of marriage to same-sex couples.

title: Zurich equality prize opened to LGBTI advocacy
description: The City of Zurich's office for gender equality announced on 4 June 2014 that its equality prize would in future also honor work on LGBTI concerns.

title: Zurich Pride draws a record 12,000 marchers for its 20th edition
description: Marking twenty years of the festival on 14 June 2014, Zurich Pride set an attendance record with 12,000 people joining the demonstration under a defiant motto.

title: UN Human Rights Council adopts a traditionalist family resolution
description: On 25 June 2014 the UN Human Rights Council passed a resolution on the protection of the family that held to the conventional heterosexual concept of family, sidelining recognition of diverse family forms.

title: St. Gallen court recognizes two men as legal parents
description: On 26 August 2014 the administrative court of the canton of St. Gallen recognized a male couple as the parents of their child, who had been born through surrogacy in the United States.

title: Der Kreis opens in Swiss cinemas
description: Stefan Haupt's film about the Zurich journal Der Kreis and the couple at its heart reached Swiss theaters on 18 September 2014.

title: Switzerland submits Der Kreis for the foreign-language Oscar
description: On 22 September 2014 Switzerland selected Der Kreis as its official entry in the Academy Awards race for best foreign-language film.

title: Council of States declines to penalize public homophobia
description: On 23 September 2014 the Swiss Council of States voted 22 to 13 against prosecuting publicly expressed homophobia, rejecting a Geneva cantonal initiative to extend the constitution's anti-racism penal norm accordingly.

title: Association queerAltern forms to support older queer people
description: queerAltern, an organization devoted to the concerns of aging LGBT people, was established on 28 October 2014.

title: Federal Council proposes opening stepchild adoption
description: On 28 November 2014 the Swiss Federal Council put forward a revision of adoption law intended to let people adopt their partner's children, a change that would cover same-sex couples.

title: Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Luzern disbands
description: The Lucerne gay working groups HALU, long the region's central gay organization, dissolved on 10 December 2014.

title: Queer youth platform renamed Milchjugend
description: On 10 March 2015 the association behind the Swiss queer youth platform adopted the name Milchjugend.

title: Der Kreis wins four Swiss Film Awards
description: At the Swiss Film Award ceremony in Geneva on 13 March 2015, Der Kreis took home four trophies.

title: Quadranti asks parliament to track anti-LGBT hate crimes
description: On 5 May 2015 National Councillor Rosmarie Quadranti of the Conservative Democratic Party filed an interpellation demanding official statistics on hate crimes motivated by sexual orientation; left unhandled for two years, it was formally written off on 16 June 2017.

title: Basel parliamentarian challenges the blood-donation ban
description: FDP National Councillor Daniel Stolz filed an interpellation on 6 May 2015 urging the Federal Council to lift the ban on blood donation by homosexual men, arguing the blanket exclusion was disproportionate.

title: Federal Supreme Court denies a male couple joint parenthood
description: On 21 May 2015 Switzerland's highest court ruled that both men of a couple could not be recognized as the legal parents of their child.

title: Operation Libero and LGBT groups petition for marriage equality
description: On 28 May 2015 the campaign group Operation Libero together with Switzerland's LGBT federations launched a petition demanding that civil marriage be opened to same-sex couples.

title: Senate committee backs the marriage-equality initiative
description: In August 2015 the legal affairs committee of the Council of States endorsed the Green Liberal Ehe für alle initiative by 7 votes to 5, following earlier approval by its National Council counterpart, keeping the plan to open marriage to same-sex couples on track.

title: Federal Council signals openness on the blood-donation ban
description: Answering the Stolz interpellation on 2 September 2015, the Swiss Federal Council responded positively in principle to ending the blanket donor exclusion, pointing to the Swiss Red Cross blood centers as the bodies that would have to amend their questionnaires.

title: Dialogai opens Refuge Genève for queer youth
description: In 2015 the Geneva organization Dialogai launched Refuge Genève, a new support service aimed at young LGBTIQ+ people.

title: Swiss voters reject the CVP marriage-definition initiative
description: In the referendum of 28 February 2016 Switzerland turned down the Christian Democratic initiative against the so-called marriage penalty; besides abolishing joint taxation, the proposal would have anchored marriage in the federal constitution as the union of a man and a woman.

title: Bern group girLs launches for young lesbians
description: In February 2016 the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Bern set up girLs, a group for young lesbian women.

title: National Council approves stepchild adoption for same-sex couples
description: On 30 May 2016 the Swiss National Council voted 127 to 60, with 2 abstentions, in favor of allowing partners in registered partnerships to adopt each other's children.

title: Swiss Red Cross moves to end the blanket MSM donor ban
description: On 20 June 2016 the Swiss Red Cross announced its intention to drop the categorical exclusion of men who have sex with men from blood donation.

title: Switzerland joins the Equal Rights Coalition
description: At the Global LGBTI Human Rights Conference held in Montevideo from 3 to 15 July 2016, Switzerland became a member of the Equal Rights Coalition, an intergovernmental alliance for the equality of trans, lesbian, gay and intersex people.

title: Tolerantia Award honors two Swiss activists
description: On 25 October 2016 in Belfast, the European Tolerantia Award for Switzerland went to Florian Vock and Jazzmin Dian Moore.

title: National LGBT conference convenes in Bern
description: On 29 October 2016 activists and organizations from across Switzerland met in Bern for a national LGBT conference.

title: LGBT+ Helpline Schweiz launches
description: The national reporting and support line LGBT+ Helpline Schweiz was set up on 14 November 2016 and opened with a public awareness campaign.

title: Swissmedic approves ending the blanket MSM donor exclusion
description: On 31 January 2017 the Swiss therapeutic products authority Swissmedic granted the Swiss Red Cross's request to abolish the categorical ban on blood donation by men who have sex with men.

title: Bern gay working groups add a politics arm
description: On 1 February 2017 the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Bern created a dedicated working group for political advocacy.

title: Parliamentary committee orders draft to outlaw anti-LGBT discrimination
description: On 19 February 2017 the National Council's legal affairs committee instructed the federal administration to prepare a penal-code amendment criminalizing discrimination based on sexual orientation as well as gender identity.

title: Basel's HABS becomes habs queer basel
description: On 28 March 2017 the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Basel adopted the name habs queer basel, presenting the change as an opening of the organization to further groups across the LGBTI spectrum.

title: Swiss Pride 2017 takes place in Bern
description: The national Pride celebration was hosted by Bern from 25 to 27 August 2017.

title: Alan David Sangines receives the Tolerantia Award
description: On 20 September 2017 the European Tolerantia Award recognized Alan David Sangines for his contributions to the LGBT community and to LGBT refugees in Switzerland.

title: New motion demands statistics on anti-LGBT hate crimes
description: On 18 September 2017 National Councillor Rosmarie Quadranti of the Conservative Democratic Party filed a fresh motion calling for the systematic recording of hate crimes based on sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or sex characteristics.

title: Zurich groups vote to build the Regenbogenhaus
description: At extraordinary general meetings on 27 and 29 November 2017, the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich and the Regenbogenhaus association resolved to realize a rainbow house within the Kalkbreite cooperative's Zollhaus project, planned as a home for many LGBTIQ* organizations and the wider community.

title: Council of States keeps the blood-donation ban
description: On 29 November 2017 the Swiss Council of States, breaking with the National Council's position, voted with 22 members against lifting the donor ban on men who have sex with men.

title: Stepchild adoption for same-sex couples takes effect
description: From 1 January 2018 same-sex partners in registered partnerships or de facto relationships could legally adopt their partner's children; the revised adoption law entered into force after no referendum was called against it.

title: Milchbar opens in Lucerne
description: In February 2018 the Milchjugend association, together with the Kopfkino collective, opened the Milchbar inside the Treibhaus youth culture house in Lucerne.

title: Luststreifen film festival goes independent
description: On 20 March 2018 the general assembly of habs queer basel confirmed the separation of its Luststreifen working group, which had run an annual queer film festival since 2008; the festival was to continue under an independent body as Luststreifen Film Festival Basel.

title: Pink Cross adopts an expanded name
description: At its general assembly on 25 March 2018, the members of Pink Cross approved renaming the national umbrella organization so that its name explicitly covers gay and bi men.

title: Coalition report documents anti-LGBT discrimination in Switzerland
description: On 4 May 2018 a broad alliance of LGBT organizations published a report showing that LGBT people in Switzerland face disproportionate levels of discrimination and violence, calling on federal and cantonal authorities to take countermeasures.
